food_name: VIA® Fair Trade Certified™ Italian Roast
aliases: Starbucks VIA Ready Brew Italian Roast

english: Starbucks VIA Ready Brew Italian Roast

serving.natural_language_portion: One stick of instant coffee (approximately 3.3 g), typically dissolved in 240 ml of hot water to prepare a single serving of black coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, instant, regular, powder
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central for generic instant coffee powder (FDC ID 171886). Values represent the dry powder form per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Coffee, instant, black, with water
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of approximately 40 for instant black coffee prepared with water, sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 2.41) / 100 ≈ 1.0.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to 1–2 sticks per day, as caffeine in instant coffee can transiently elevate systolic blood pressure by 5–10 mmHg, particularly in caffeine-naive or salt-sensitive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index when consumed black
type2_diabetes.guidance: Black instant coffee has a low glycemic index (approximately 40) and negligible glycemic load per serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes provided no sugar or high-GI creamers are added.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Moderate consumption of up to 3–4 cups per day is generally considered safe for most individuals with cardiovascular disease; however, those with arrhythmias or uncontrolled hypertension should consult a clinician before regular use.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA® Fair Trade Certified™ Italian Roast is a premium single-serve instant coffee product sold in convenient stick-format sachets, each containing approximately 3.3 g of finely milled, microground 100% arabica coffee powder. The Italian Roast designation indicates a very dark roast level, producing a bold, roasty, and slightly sweet flavour profile with low acidity and a full-bodied mouthfeel when dissolved in hot water. Unlike conventional spray-dried instant coffees, Starbucks VIA uses a proprietary microgrind technology that preserves the aromatic oils and flavour compounds characteristic of freshly brewed espresso-style coffee. Preparation is simple: dissolve one stick in approximately 240 ml of hot water (around 90–95 °C) and stir for 10 seconds. The product is made with Fair Trade Certified beans, ensuring ethical sourcing standards for coffee farmers. Nutritionally, the dry powder per 100 g is energy-dense (352 kcal) due to its concentrated form, with significant protein (12.2 g), dietary fibre (15.8 g), and virtually no sugar. When prepared as a beverage, the caloric contribution per serving is minimal (approximately 12 kcal). The coffee is a rich source of potassium (3530 mg/100 g powder), magnesium (325 mg/100 g), and niacin (28.2 mg/100 g). Clinically, black instant coffee has a low glycemic index (~40) and negligible glycemic load, making it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar. Caffeine content warrants moderation for those with hypertension or cardiovascular conditions. The product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is widely available in Singapore through Starbucks outlets and delivery platforms.
common_user_queries: How to prepare Starbucks VIA sticks

confidence_reason: Nutrient data for instant coffee powder is sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 171886), an approved primary source. Glycemic index for instant black coffee is from the University of Sydney GI Database. Multilingual names are provided in accordance with Singaporean context policy. Product identity is confirmed from the field survey data input record referencing Starbucks.
notes: per_100g.sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain instant coffee powder. per_100g.cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as coffee contains no animal-derived sterols. per_100g.vitamin_c_mg is naturally zero as roasting destroys ascorbic acid. per_100g.vitamin_a_mcg_rae is naturally zero as coffee beans contain no retinol or beta-carotene. per_100g.vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as this is a plant-derived product. per_100g.folate_mcg_dfe is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. per_100g.vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero as coffee is not a source of vitamin D. per_100g.monounsaturated_fat_g and per_100g.trans_fat_g are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (40 × 2.41 g carbohydrate per stick) / 100 ≈ 1.0. The per_100g values represent the dry instant coffee powder; when prepared as a beverage, nutrient concentrations are substantially diluted. The product is sold in 12-stick packs at SGD 16.70 via field survey data at Starbucks One Fullerton.
